BST:
class Solution {
public:
TreeNode* lowestCommonAncestor(TreeNode* root, TreeNode* p, TreeNode* q) { //BST
if(!root) return NULL
// a: Root,b:Smaller one,c:Larger one
int a=root->val
int b=p->val<q->val?p->val:q->val
int c=b==p->val?q->val:p->val
//Check if i am the LCA,if yes return
if(a>=b && a<=c) return root
//Check with BST property for LCA
if(a<b) return lowestCommonAncestor(root->right,p,q)
if(a>c) return lowestCommonAncestor(root->left,p,q)

27,2,LEARNING,Sun Jun 07 2026 15:25:48 GMT+0300 (التوقيت العربي الرسمي),DP: On every index u can either

column1: (buy/skip) if !holding a stock orr
(sell/skip) if holding a stock.
Make a DP(n,2) nearly 1d dp sol.
Also in this tabulation works from calculating from end till start as we always want dp[i+1][0/1].
Also since in all iterations we only need dp[i+1], we can just keep track of two vectors curr(2) and next(2) and when calculating from back we can always do next=curr. O(1) spacee.
DP:
class Solution {
public:
int prof(vector<int>& pr,vector<vector<int>> &dp,int i,int hold){
if(dp[i][hold]!=-1) return dp[i][hold]
if(hold){
dp[i][hold]=max(pr[i]+prof(pr,dp,i+1,0),prof(pr,dp,i+1,1))
else dp[i][hold]=max(-1*pr[i]+prof(pr,dp,i+1,1),prof(pr,dp,i+1,0))
return dp[i][hold]
int maxProfit(vector<int>& pr) {
int n=pr.size()
vector<vector<int>> dp(n+1,vector<int>(2,-1))
return prof(pr,dp,0,0)

First thought: The target sum expression is just sum of all numbers with some being negative and some being positive.
So divide into two groups pos and neg.
So we need those grouos such that sum of pos-neg is tagret.
Also pos+neg is total sum.
So by these equations we get pos=(sum+target)/2 which should be even.
Now just count the ways so that subset sum equals pos
Also 0's play a crucial role here so our dp init is just dp[0][0]=1 not all dp[i][0]=1 bcz suppose i have 2 0's so no. of ways to mae target 0 with having all these 0's is 4,(+0-0,+0+0,-0-0,-0+0).

35,2,LEARNING,Sun Jun 14 2026 15:49:56 GMT+0300 (التوقيت العربي الرسمي),Done in 2 pass(Optimal sol).

column1: First pass start from left and check curr and gt its left neighbour and if yes give more candies that left ne.
Next pass do same with starting from right and checking right neighbours and if it has less candies than neighbour then give 1 candy more than the neighbour.
The intution is when checking ne it should have comoleted its count and finalised so we do it this way.
The brute force way takes n2 time where for each elemnt we check both its neighbours and inc candy accordingly this is one pass.
These passes are done until the array stays the same.(use while(changed) loop).",Wed Jun 24 2026 15:49:56 GMT+0300 (التوقيت العربي الرسمي),1,485,0,,,
36,2,LEARNING,Sun Jun 14 2026 16:22:19 GMT+0300 (التوقيت العربي الرسمي),"Use a monotonic stack.
When you try to push a elemnt and the top is lesser than elmnt then we have found the nxt greater for top so set it and pop and repeat until top is greter or equal to elmnt.
This ensures stack is mono dec upwards.
Intution: suppose i have 30 28 26 27 40
so st: 30 28 26 then 27 is nxt grt for 26 so pop so now st: 30 28 27 and now 40 is next grt for all so all updated
The thing is middle ones get updated with their nxt grt..
O(n) approach as a elemnt is pused and popped only once.",Sat Jun 20 2026 16:22:19 GMT+0300 (التوقيت العربي الرسمي),1,443,0,,,
